module1.clean_text:
Hospitalization report
The child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] and a half years old from and living in Oran, followed since the age of [AGE] for cystic fibrosis with dual respiratory and digestive phenotypic expression, readmitted for moderate exacerbation of Pseudomonas Aeruginosa. HISTORY:
Family:
- Father: [AGE] years old; BES. - Mother: [AGE] years old, BES. - 2nd degree consanguinity. - Siblings:
1 – girl; aged [AGE] years (screened, negative). 2 – our patient. 3- a brother [AGE] years old, followed at our level for cystic fibrosis. Personal:
- Pregnancy: well monitored. - Delivery: by VB at term. - Apgar good at birth. - Birth weight: 3 kg100. - Meconium: emitted before 24 hours. - Breastfeeding for 15 days then artificial breastfeeding. - Food diversification: 07 [AGE] - vitamin D received at 1 and 6 months. - DPM: considered normal according to age. History of illness:
Primary diagnosis: cystic fibrosis. Associated diagnoses: Severe malnutrition with RSP, Concept of epilepsy/Convulsions
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] Age of diagnosis: [AGE] Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics
-2nd degree consanguinity. -Concept of death in the family. -clinic
-Chronic cough evolving since neonatal age. -Bronchial congestion. -greasy stool. -Para clinical:
-Positive sweat test twice. - Fecal elastase (02/07/2025): 0.3 ug/g (severe exocrine pancreatic insufficiency)
- Genetic study:
Hospitalization history:
Processing in progress
Colistin one month per month
Respiratory physiotherapy twice a day. Nebulization by SSI
Poly vitamin fiadek
Zomax syrups 1 day/02 days
Creon 12000 IU: 1 to 2 gel per meal (before meals)
Therapeutic compliance: Good
HISTORY OF ILLNESS in relation to the reason for hospitalization: [DATE] start of the signs dates back 15 days previously with accentuation of the cough becoming bothersome with asthenia and reduction in appetite; an ECBC of sputum returned positive for Pseudomonas Aeruginosa, hence his hospitalization. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 10/28/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 13 kg (-2.2 SD). Size: 91 CM (-3.375 DS). Marked stature delay
BMI: 15.69% (+0.1SD) . P/A: 76.5%; P/T: 98%, therefore mild malnutrition. General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.6° C, mild malnutrition. Skin examination: CV (BCG), good mucosal skin coloring, no jaundice or hemorrhagic syndrome. ENT exam: clean. Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 59 c/, fits of wet cough, auscultation, fine bilateral crackles, vesicular murmurs well transmitted in 2 pulmonary fields. - Signs of struggles: TSC, Spo2: 98% under AA. - No signs of chronic respiratory failure (thoracic deformation, clubbing)
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 154 b/min. Auscultation: no murmur or BSA, heart sounds were clearly audible. Abdominal examination, soft distended abdomen, 03 to 04 loose fatty stools, no signs of dehydration, free hernia orifices, no rectal prolapse. Diminished appetite, disturbed sleep. preserved diuresis. Examination of other devices without particularity. Support:
On the respiratory level
Nebulization with SSH and B2 before respiratory physiotherapy. Twice-daily respiratory physiotherapy
Bi IV antibiotic therapy:
Fortum 200mg/kg/day. Amikacin 25 mg/kg/day IV for 10 days. Colistin in nebulization: 01 Million U 02*days
Zomax 1 day/2 days
Nutritionally:
Interview with the dietitian. Growth curve. Calorie increase to 170%. polyvitamin supplementation: {vit D/2 months Vit K/15 days Vit E/15 days} and iron deficiency. Creon 12000 iu 01 capsule 10/day (12,000 iu/kg/day)
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 10/28/25
NFS: GB: 15.32 element, PNN: 8610; lymphocyte: 2980
HB: 13.1g/dl; MCV: 79.7fl; CCMH: 35
PLQ: 325,000 el/mm 3. CRP: 4.7(Negative)
PCT: 0.07ng/l
Ionogram: Na: 133.9 mmol/l K: 3.83 mmol/l Cl: 104.2 mmol/l
Albumin: 40.7 g/l Creat: 5.36 mg/l Urea: 0.11 g/l Protein Total: 72.5 g/l
Ferritin: 21.97 ng/ml 25 OH Vit D: 20.1 ng/ml
Repercussions and focus of the disease:
On the respiratory level:
no sign of chronic respiratory failure “no clubbing, no cyanosis, no thoracic deformity”. no sign of chronic cor pulmonale.
Heart echo (11/2024): without anomaly
Nocturnal saturation curve and respiratory rate without correct O2 (12/11/2024)
ECBC of sputum:
Bacterial culture:
Pseudomonas Aeruginosa
Antibiogram 01:
R au: amox; amox + clavulanic acid; cefoxitin; cefixime, cefotaxim, nitrofurantoin
S au: ceftazidime; imipenem; ciprofloxacin, levofloxacin; Gentamicin; fosfomycin
EFR + walking test as soon as age permits. Arterial blood gas: 06/16/25
ph: 7.324 in2:91 mm hg pco2 39.3 mm hg without abnormality. Chest CT 02/24/2025: bronchial syndrome made of thickening of the proximal bronchial walls without bronchiectasis associated with mediatisnal and right hilar adenomegaly. On the hemodynamic level
no signs of heart failure (no edema or HPMG, TJ
no signs of chronic cor pulmonale. heart echo 11/2024: good systolic function FE 65% no PAH. On the Hepatobiliary level:
- no jaundice, no notion of digestive bleeding, absence of hepatomegaly. -Correct liver assessment. -Abdominopelvic ultrasound: homogeneous hepatomegaly, discreet left pyelic ectasia. On the Digestive level:
absence of signs of intestinal obstruction: preserved transit, no rectal bleeding, but soft distended abdomen. absence of constipation, no sign of intestinal intussusception. absence of the clinical picture suggestive of GERD. absence of rectal prolapse. Homogeneous HPMG abdominopelvic ultrasound, discreet left pyelic ectasia. Nutritionally:
Weight: 13 kg (-2.2 SD). Size: 91 CM (-3.375 DS). BMI: 15.7% (-2.24DS) . P/A: 76.5%; P/T: 98% therefore mild malnutrition
preserved daily ration. Metabolic assessment:
Blood sugar: 0.83 g/l HbA1C: 5.95%. correct nutritional assessment except hypovitaminosis D
Growth curve. Evolution during hospitalization:
On the respiratory level:
Regression of signs of struggle, complete disappearance of cough. Child stayed for 15 days having received Bi antibiotic therapy: fortum for 14 days, amikacin for 10 days. Circumcision done on 11/10/2025: at our level, passed without incident
CONCLUSION:
The child ABDELLAOUI MOHAMED YANIS aged 04 and a half years old originating and residing in Oran, from a 2nd degree consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 03 months for cystic fibrosis with double phenotypic respiratory and digestive expression, complicated by malnutrition moderate PE readmitted for moderate exacerbation with Pseudomonas Aerogenosa. Child discharged after good clinical and biological evolution, and discharge weight: 14.3 kg
An anti-flu vaccination was given on 12/11/2025
Inspection appointment on: 02/01/2026
To collect / To do:
Assessment of immune deficiency (launched on 11/11/2025)
Aspergillus serology (launched on 11/11/2025)
CT of the sinuses (appointment 11/26/2025)
Echocardiography
Abdominopelvic ultrasound
Thoracic CT with injection to be done in 03 months
ECBC with antibiogram
Plan a nighttime saturation curve for the next hospitalization
Resident of the department: Dr Djoudi Assistant of the department: Dr OUSSALAH

Family:
- Father: 41 years old; BES. - Mother: 27 years old, BES. - 2nd degree consanguinity. - Siblings:
1 – girl; aged 08 years (screened, negative). 2 – our patient. 3- a brother 02 years old, followed at our level for cystic fibrosis. Personal:
- Pregnancy: well monitored. - Delivery: by VB at term. - Apgar good at birth. - Birth weight: 3 kg100. - Meconium: emitted before 24 hours. - Breastfeeding for 15 days then artificial breastfeeding. - Food diversification: 07 months. - vitamin D received at 1 and 6 months. - DPM: considered normal according to age. Cardiovascular examination: HR: 154 b/min. Auscultation: no murmur or BSA, heart sounds were clearly audible.
